verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member that localizes to the mitochondrial membrane and cytoplasm. During apoptosis, BAX translocates to the mitochondrial outer membrane where it forms oligomeric pores (BAX/BAK complexes) and interacts with VDAC to mediate cytochrome c release. It is not a plasma-membrane-anchored protein and does not reach the cell-surface outer leaflet via its own mechanism. While some reports describe BAX translocation events during apoptosis, these are mitochondrial-membrane events, not plasma-membrane surface accessibility. No therapeutic modalities successfully target BAX on the cell-surface exterior.
